title: Unlocking Offshore Freshened Groundwater
author: OFF-SOURCE
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This technical report explores the potential of Offshore Freshened Groundwater (OFG) as an alternative water resource for water-stressed coastal regions. 
  Coastal aquifers are heavily relied upon but increasingly strained due to urbanization, population growth, and climate change, creating an urgent need for supplementary water sources. OFG, fresh groundwater stored in sub-seafloor aquifers, presents an unconventional solution to address this demand. The report reviews cost-effective infrastructure and logistical considerations for OFG extraction, assessing it alongside conventional sources like desalination. Key factors include water quality, treatment needs, extraction and transport logistics, and site-specific conditions. Notably, synergies with the Oil & Gas industry could reduce initial infrastructure costs and bring advanced expertise in offshore operations to OFG extraction. While OFG offers potential benefits, challenges remain in areas like environmental impact, aquifer recharge rates, legal frameworks, and sustainable resource management. This report emphasizes that, while OFG could supplement water supplies, significant technical and regulatory steps are required. The findings suggest a path forward through strategic investments in research, technological innovation, and collaborative partnerships across sectors. OFG thus represents a promising option for enhancing coastal water resilience in an era of increasing water scarcity.
